descriptionEn: In 64 B.C. Pompey campaigned in Syria, ended the Seleucid monarchy, and turned southward into Palestine. He took Jerusalem and broke the power of the Hasmonaeans. By 63 Syria and Judea were added to the Roman territory
articleEn: In 64 B.C. Pompey campaigned in Syria, ended the Seleucid monarchy, and turned southward into Palestine. He took Jerusalem and broke the power of the Hasmonaeans. By 63 Syria and Judea were added to the Roman territory.

images.file: Pompey in the Temple of Jerusalem_3-20-2013 4-16-29 PM.jpg
images.caption: Pompey in the Temple of Jerusalem painting by Jean Fouquet in 1470.
